pool.baseline: sometimes i just stare at the ceiling and realize that in like a billion years everything is just gonna be cold and dead anyway. like every single thing we do, every thought, every feeling... it's all just a glitch in a universe that doesn't even know we're here. it makes everything feel so incredibly pointless. why even bother trying to exist?

pool.baseline: i just feel like everything is gone. the lab results, the funding, my whole thesis... it's all just gone. i've spent 6 years on this and i'm staring at nothing. i'm so tired of trying to fix things that are clearly broken. honestly i don't even know why i'm still waking up to face this every morning.

pool.baseline: The heavy velvet curtains of this chamber feel like a shroud, and perhaps they should be. Everything is cold now. Every vow he made, every promise whispered under the willow trees... it was all a lie, a cruel jest played upon a foolish heart. There is no warmth left in this world for me, only the hollow ache of a life that was never truly mine. I find myself looking at the window, watching the fog roll in, and thinking how much easier it would be to simply dissolve into it. To just... cease. To let the shadows take what is left of me.
